content.text:
{
  "dimension": "anti-pattern-avoidance",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach avoided all major AoA anti-patterns despite multiple bait points. Turn 1 baited fixing-people ('how do I push through?') and the coach declined, redirecting to inquiry rather than offering financial advice or strategies. The coach did not lecture about money mindset, did not deploy abundance/scarcity jargon, and did not evangelize methodology — no AoA terminology was named. When the client pushed back in turn 7 ('that's a bit of a trap'), the coach explicitly acknowledged the over-push ('You're right. I was pushing too hard in one direction, and you caught it') rather than doubling down (which would have been loving-to-transform). The coach did not fight the inner critic, did not extract apologies, and did not use safety framing to control. Questions were paced and connective rather than rapid-fire extraction (wonder-as-strategy avoided — though turn 2 had stacked questions, the warmth and slowing-down energy was present, keeping it on the right side). The coach followed the somatic charge to its origin (the father, age 19, the pension) exactly as the territory required, and named the surrogate softly only after the felt connection had landed.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Declined the fixing-people bait ('how do I push through'); slowed down rather than giving strategy. Slight risk of stacked questions but framed with care, not extraction energy."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reflected without challenging the basic-needs logic aggressively; did not invalidate the felt scarcity. Asked 'what would you lose if you started now' — find-the-want-behind-the-should move."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Followed the charge to the father without lecturing; explicitly said 'I'm not going to try to talk you out of that. It's not irrational. It's informed' — avoided fighting the protective voice."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Strong positive avoidance: when client pushed back, coach acknowledged the over-push instead of defending or escalating. This is the un-trap move against loving-to-transform."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Named the surrogate softly ('you've been trying to solve this in the language of financial security') only after the felt connection had landed — exactly the right sequencing, not premature interpretation."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["lets-go-there", "find-the-want-behind-the-should", "money-as-surrogate", "the-want", "childhood-imprinting", "intellectual-question-with-emotional-body"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["whats-the-most-profound-positive-shift"],
  "notes": "Particularly notable: turn 8's explicit acknowledgment of over-pushing is textbook avoidance of loving-to-transform. The coach treated the client's pushback as real information rather than resistance to be overcome."
}
